seoTitle: Kergunyah South vs Kernot — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Kergunyah South and Kernot. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Kergunyah South vs Kernot: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Kergunyah South and Kernot.
heading: Kergunyah South vs Kernot — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kergunyah South, Victoria and Kernot, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Kernot has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Kergunyah South (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kergunyah South or Kernot safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kergunyah South has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Kernot scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kergunyah South scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. Kernot scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kergunyah South or Kernot?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Kernot has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
